Beginner.meaning: To take care of someone or something.
Beginner.example: She looks after her little brother every evening.

Beginner.meaning: To start a device or machine.
Beginner.example: Please turn on the laptop before the session starts.
I wake up early to prepare for work.
Please sit down while we set up the projector.
Can you pick up the package from reception?
She put on her headset before the call.

Beginner.meaning: To enter a system by typing details.
Beginner.example: Log in to the portal to check your tasks.

Beginner.meaning: To stop a device or machine.
Beginner.example: Turn off the AC before leaving the room.

Beginner.meaning: To rise from bed or a sitting position.
Beginner.example: He gets up early to exercise.

Beginner.meaning: To leave home for social activity or work.
Beginner.example: We go out for lunch on Fridays.
Please come in and take a seat.
I get on the bus every morning at 8 AM.
We get off at the second stop.

Beginner.meaning: To store something in its place.
Beginner.example: Put away your laptop after the meeting.
I’m looking for my ID card.

Beginner.meaning: To direct your eyes toward something.
Beginner.example: Look at the board to read instructions.

Beginner.meaning: To record information on paper or device.
Beginner.example: Write down the meeting time in your notebook.
Turn up the volume, I can't hear you.
Turn down the AC, it's too cold in here.
Put out the candle before leaving.
I will pay for the workspace pass online.
I will call you back after the stand-up meeting.
Please check in at the reception desk.
We checked out of the hotel at 11 AM.
Fill out the application form online.
He pointed out an error in the report.
They put up posters for the workshop.
She tried on the headphones before buying them.
Hurry up or we’ll be late for the call.

Beginner.meaning: To return in the direction you came.
Beginner.example: We forgot the files, so we turned back.

Intermediate.meaning: To understand something or solve a problem.
Intermediate.example: We finally figured out the reason for the bug.
They put off the workshop until tomorrow.

Intermediate.meaning: To mention a topic in conversation.
Intermediate.example: She brought up team bonding activities in the meeting.
Despite challenges, they carried on with development.
We are looking into the authentication issue.

Intermediate.meaning: To solve a problem or calculate.
Intermediate.example: We worked out a better pricing strategy.
They set up a new testing server today.

Intermediate.meaning: To reduce the amount of something.
Intermediate.example: We cut down costs by switching providers.
I look forward to our product launch.
He came up with a creative app feature.

Intermediate.meaning: To perform or complete a task.
Intermediate.example: The team carried out user testing.
Let’s go over the plan once more.
She broke down the concept into easy steps.
Please fill in the attendance sheet.
I will drop off the laptop at the office.

Intermediate.meaning: To notice or understand something subtle.
Intermediate.example: He picked up on the new design approach quickly.

Intermediate.meaning: To support or make a copy.
Intermediate.example: Always back up your project files.

Intermediate.meaning: To stop or interrupt something or someone.
Intermediate.example: The call was cut off due to network issues.
This feature will bring about better customer retention.

Intermediate.meaning: To reach the same level as others.
Intermediate.example: He worked hard to catch up with the syllabus.

Intermediate.meaning: To handle a situation or problem.
Intermediate.example: We need to deal with user complaints quickly.

Intermediate.meaning: To finish or use all of something.
Intermediate.example: We ran out of printer paper during the event.

Intermediate.meaning: To open or access something on a device.
Intermediate.example: Pull up the report on the dashboard.
Let's sort out the task priorities first.

Intermediate.meaning: To start a new activity or hobby.
Intermediate.example: She took up learning data analytics.
He pointed out the missing field in the form.
Please hand in your project before Friday.
We went through all customer comments.
Look up the API documentation before coding.

Intermediate.meaning: To stay updated or match pace.
Intermediate.example: Engineers must keep up with new technologies.

Advanced.example: She took over the project leadership.

Advanced.meaning: To face a problem or meet unexpectedly.
Advanced.example: We ran into deployment issues last night.

Advanced.meaning: To gradually remove or end something.
Advanced.example: The team is phasing out legacy systems.

Advanced.meaning: To dismiss employees for economic reasons.
Advanced.example: Several companies laid off staff during the downturn.
Strong leaders always follow through on commitments.
We carried out a full audit before launch.

Advanced.meaning: To reinforce information or support someone.
Advanced.example: Data reports backed up his proposal.
We cut back on marketing budgets temporarily.

Advanced.meaning: To use success as a base for future progress.
Advanced.example: We built on last year's achievements to scale operations.
The merger was called off due to regulatory concerns.
The startup pulled through its financial crisis.
Let's zero in on customer retention strategies.
We fended off a cyber attack last quarter.
Security updates rooted out hidden vulnerabilities.
We worked around the API limitation with custom logic.

Advanced.meaning: To increase size, capacity, or production.
Advanced.example: The company is scaling up infrastructure for global traffic.
Analysts zoomed in on user churn metrics.

Advanced.meaning: To launch a product or update.
Advanced.example: We rolled out the new dashboard last week.
The team ramped up hiring in Q2.
Investors bailed out the failing startup.

Advanced.meaning: To identify one person or thing from a group.
Advanced.example: The manager singled out the best performer for recognition.
We are phasing in the new authentication system.
Let's wind up the meeting and finalize action items.

Advanced.meaning: To check or continue something later.
Advanced.example: I will follow up on the client request tomorrow.

Advanced.meaning: To get involved to help or intervene.
Advanced.example: The VP stepped in to resolve the dispute.

Advanced.meaning: To get rid of through activity.
Advanced.example: I work off stress by running every evening.

Advanced.meaning: To remove by drawing a line through.
Advanced.example: Cross out the items already completed.
They went ahead with the deployment after QA approval.

Advanced.meaning: To hold tightly to an idea or belief.
Advanced.example: Some teams cling to old workflows even when better tools exist.

Advanced.meaning: To be explained by or based on something.
Advanced.example: Success ultimately comes down to consistent effort.
